chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper explicitly provides working links to the source code and artifacts at the end of Section VIII (Conclusion), citing a Zenodo DOI and a GitHub repository URL.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on detailed cycle-accurate simulations using gem5 v22.0.0.1 with custom core models; there is no mention of physical hardware testbeds or large-scale cloud deployments.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: Section V ('Sphere of Replication') contains a clear, explicit definition of the fault model, specifying that ParaVerser detects hard and soft errors within the CPU sphere (registers, LSQ, logic) but assumes ECC/parity for caches and memory, explicitly stating what components are covered and what assumptions are made.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of trade-offs in Section VII, quantifying exact performance slowdowns (geomean), energy overheads (static/dynamic via McPAT), area overheads (pixel counts), and NoC sensitivity impacts across various configurations.

major_weakness: The experimental evaluation is conducted entirely through simulation (gem5) without any physical hardware validation or large-scale deployment.
